name: Drug Induced Liver Injury
description: This test case evaluates the ability to identify inpatients with acute drug-induced liver injury (DILI). The phenotype requires exposure to a suspect drug within 3 months prior to liver injury with elevated liver enzymes meeting DILI thresholds.
prompt: Find all hospitalized patients who have experienced acute drug-induced liver injury, characterized by significantly elevated liver enzymes (ALT greater than 5 times upper limit of normal or ALP greater than 2 times upper limit of normal) following medication exposure.

required_codes.display: Alanine aminotransferase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
Alkaline phosphatase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma
